improvements_list.description: Added proportional weight self-verification with actual line counts (59.6% PART 0, 24.1% PART II, 16.3% PART III+IV), making the 60/25/15 target concrete and falsifiable rather than aspirational.

Fixed 19 bare cross-module references by adding explicit module qualifications (e.g., 'element-specifications module', 'guidance-operations module'), directly addressing INV-012 cross-module isolation requirements.

Added four new DO NOT constraints in modularization module: negative_specs field omission, maintains field omission, silent file skipping during assembly, and deferring reference conversion during migration. Strengthens INV-017 compliance for the modularization subsystem.
Added meta-standard adaptation note clarifying how DDIS applies its own prescriptions reflexively, making the self-bootstrapping contract explicit rather than implicit.

Added 'for that subsystem' qualifier to INV-017 declaration, aligning the constitutional declaration with the module-level definition and resolving a declaration-definition faithfulness gap (INV-015).
Added INV-015 verification note under compact glossary confirming declaration-definition synchronization has been audited.
Corrected §0.11/§0.12 nesting relationship and fixed §6.1.1 section attribution in the Section Map, improving navigability and reducing risk of implementer confusion.

Checked off 5 modular self-conformance items with concrete verification notes (e.g., 'verified RALPH iter-2: 19 bare refs fixed across 3 modules', '30/31 faithful, INV-017 qualifier added'), converting aspirational TODOs into audited confirmations.

gate_failures.note: Requires external implementation pilot; cannot be assessed through spec editing alone
ddis_assemble.sh and ddis_validate.sh not yet implemented; tooling gates remain open

remaining_gaps.description: Gates 6, 7, and M-3 require tooling and external pilots that cannot be satisfied by further spec text editing. These represent the ceiling for editorial improvement.
Updated to '1,144–1,442 lines' but actual line counts should be re-verified after each assembly to prevent drift from the ≤1,500 line budget (INV-013).
§0.8.2 self-verification is a snapshot; no automated mechanism ensures proportional weights remain accurate as content evolves across iterations.

rationale: v2 delivers 8 substantive improvements with 0 regressions, demonstrating continued quality gains. However, the improvements are predominantly cleanup and verification work (cross-reference fixups, TODO check-offs, negative spec additions) rather than structural or conceptual advances. The delta from v1 (~87) to v2 (~90) is approximately 3 points — meaningful but narrowing. The remaining gaps (external validation tooling, automated verification) cannot be resolved through further spec editing; they require implementation artifacts outside the RALPH editorial loop. The spec has reached its editorial quality ceiling. Further iterations would yield diminishing returns: the low-hanging fruit is harvested, cross-references are clean, negative specs meet INV-017 thresholds, and the self-bootstrapping contract is explicit and verified. Continued iteration risks introducing churn without proportional benefit.
